name: Phillips, Oliver W.
clubs: Representative Prestonian Literary Society in Inter-Society Oratorical Contest, '26
Representative Prestonian Literary Society in Inter-Society Debate, '27
Representative of College in Inter-Collegiate Oratorical Contest, '27
description: Oliver is a hard worker with a serious outlook on life—except where the fair sex is concerned. Since he has made a success of his years at S. T. C., we feel sure that in later life he will continue to "deliver the goods."
text: Oliver is a hard worker with a serious outlook on life—except where the fair sex is concerned. Since he has made a success of his years at S. T. C., we feel sure that in later life he will continue to "deliver the goods."

name: Thompson, Rubie John
description: To know Rubie is to realize how true it is that precious things are done up in small packages.
text: To know Rubie is to realize how true it is that precious things are done up in small packages.

name: Lockard, Annie Kate
description: Annie Kate always has a cheerful smile and a friendly word for everyone. In fact, her genial personality has won her many friends.
text: Annie Kate always has a cheerful smile and a friendly word for everyone. In fact, her genial personality has won her many friends.

name: Hilbun, Roy A.
motto: "The surest way not to fail is to determine to succeed."
description: Hilbun entered the State Teachers College in December, 1924, with a determination to get a college education. He has accomplished his purpose, proved himself a worthy student; and he possesses the friendship of all who know him.
text:
"The surest way not to fail is to determine to succeed."
Hilbun entered the State Teachers College in December, 1924, with a determination to get a college education. He has accomplished his purpose, proved himself a worthy student; and he possesses the friendship of all who know him.

name: Ewell, Willie Evelyn
motto:
"Better be small and shine
Than large and cast a shadow."
description: Versatile and conscientious, Willie is a student of whom S. T. C. is proud.
text:
"Better be small and shine
Than large and cast a shadow."
Versatile and conscientious, Willie is a student of whom S. T. C. is proud.

name: East, Mildred
motto:
"Heart on her lips and soul within her eyes,
Soft as her clime, and sunny as her skies."
text:
"Heart on her lips and soul within her eyes,
Soft as her clime, and sunny as her skies."

name: Saucier, Irvin Albert
motto: "Know Mississippi Better"
description: Saucier has contributed much to the musical life of State Teachers College. In addition to his musical ability he has a quiet, unassuming personality, and is a diligent student.
text:
"Know Mississippi Better"
Saucier has contributed much to the musical life of State Teachers College. In addition to his musical ability he has a quiet, unassuming personality, and is a diligent student.

name: Hostetler, Mary Linn
description: Mary Linn, a charming and lovable little brunette, never frowning on the world or shirking her duties, makes glad the hearts of all her associates. A pure and serviceable life is the coveted end, And honest duty performed cheerfully never brings disappointment.
text: Mary Linn, a charming and lovable little brunette, never frowning on the world or shirking her duties, makes glad the hearts of all her associates. A pure and serviceable life is the coveted end, And honest duty performed cheerfully never brings disappointment.

name: Young, Margaret Mary
clubs: Senior Class Representative Home Economics Club, '28
description: Possessing such qualities as patience, wisdom, humor, sincerity, and grace, combined with noble purposes and high idealism, she has endeared herself to the hearts of all those who know her. In class work and student activities, her ability and popularity are attested by the excellent achievements and many honors that have been bestowed upon her. A miniature of loveliness with culture and grace, Summed up and mirrored in a beautiful face.
text: Possessing such qualities as patience, wisdom, humor, sincerity, and grace, combined with noble purposes and high idealism, she has endeared herself to the hearts of all those who know her. In class work and student activities, her ability and popularity are attested by the excellent achievements and many honors that have been bestowed upon her. A miniature of loveliness with culture and grace, Summed up and mirrored in a beautiful face.

name: King, John Michael
motto: "God, give me hills to climb And strength for climbing."
description: These lines bespeak the quiet determination, the calm perseverance, the eagerness for accomplishment that is his.
text:
"God, give me hills to climb And strength for climbing."
These lines bespeak the quiet determination, the calm perseverance, the eagerness for accomplishment that is his.

name: Davis, Lucile Grace
motto: "Happy, thoughtful, kind and true, There is no favor she will not do."
clubs: Corresponding Secretary B. Y. P. U., '28
description: Lucile, who came to us from Blue Mountain College, has proved to be a good student and a loyal friend. She seems very quiet, modest and reserved; but those who know her best find that she is full of fun and always ready for a joke.
text:
"Happy, thoughtful, kind and true, There is no favor she will not do."
Lucile, who came to us from Blue Mountain College, has proved to be a good student and a loyal friend. She seems very quiet, modest and reserved; but those who know her best find that she is full of fun and always ready for a joke.

name: Burnet, Eugenia
motto: "Take one ounce of originality, two cups of loyalty, along with one cup of laughter, stir thoroughly, and to this add four teaspoons of pep, a pinch of temper, and some corn flowers to color the eyes. After baking in the oven of 'congeniality' sprinkle well with all good traits, and you will have—'Gene.'"
text: "Take one ounce of originality, two cups of loyalty, along with one cup of laughter, stir thoroughly, and to this add four teaspoons of pep, a pinch of temper, and some corn flowers to color the eyes. After baking in the oven of 'congeniality' sprinkle well with all good traits, and you will have—'Gene.'"

name: White, W. L.
motto: "A man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a Heaven for?"
description: White, a faithful worker and a true friend, seeks opportunities for service, ignoring personal gain. Such a man as this tries to make the world a better place in which to live. As president of the Senior Class, his duties have been many, but he has performed them well. We predict for him a brilliant future.
text:
"A man's reach should exceed his grasp, or what's a Heaven for?"
White, a faithful worker and a true friend, seeks opportunities for service, ignoring personal gain. Such a man as this tries to make the world a better place in which to live. As president of the Senior Class, his duties have been many, but he has performed them well. We predict for him a brilliant future.

name: Peters, Eddie Inez
motto: "She looks wise and acts wise, And always seems to smile; And when it comes to doing things, She's made of the stuff worth while."
clubs: Publicity Chairman Y. W. C. A., '26, '27
description: In her quiet unassuming way, Eddie has made a splendid college record. Her perseverance, originality, and dependableness will win for her success in any undertaking.
text:
"She looks wise and acts wise, And always seems to smile; And when it comes to doing things, She's made of the stuff worth while."
In her quiet unassuming way, Eddie has made a splendid college record. Her perseverance, originality, and dependableness will win for her success in any undertaking.

name: Owings, Addison Davis
description: There are hermit souls that live withdrawn In the place of their self-content; There are souls like stars that dwell apart, In a fellowless firmament. There are pioneer souls that blaze their paths Where highways never ran; But let me live by the side of the road And be a friend to man.
text: There are hermit souls that live withdrawn In the place of their self-content; There are souls like stars that dwell apart, In a fellowless firmament. There are pioneer souls that blaze their paths Where highways never ran; But let me live by the side of the road And be a friend to man.

name: Scrivener, Bert
description: Bert, with her jolly laugh and high spirits, is like a Swiss peasant girl with whirling bright skirts, keeping in rhythm with an old folk dance.
text: Bert, with her jolly laugh and high spirits, is like a Swiss peasant girl with whirling bright skirts, keeping in rhythm with an old folk dance.

name: Lyda, Daughdrell
description: Her charming personality has won for Lydia many friends. She is an excellent student and possesses rare sincerity and determination.
text: Her charming personality has won for Lydia many friends. She is an excellent student and possesses rare sincerity and determination.

name: Mildred, Hill
description: Mildred, a true, sympathetic friend, is never too busy to share joy or sorrow. Those who claim her friendship value it most highly. A hand that's quick, small kindly deeds to do, Courage enough to see the battle through, To fail today—tomorrow rise to try.
text: Mildred, a true, sympathetic friend, is never too busy to share joy or sorrow. Those who claim her friendship value it most highly. A hand that's quick, small kindly deeds to do, Courage enough to see the battle through, To fail today—tomorrow rise to try.

name: Triplett, Katie Mae
description: She's true to her work, her words, and her friends. Those who know her best appreciate her strength of character, honesty, and sincerity. She fills all offices with dignity and efficiency. She is a typical all-round student. Girls of her type can never be forgotten.
text: She's true to her work, her words, and her friends. Those who know her best appreciate her strength of character, honesty, and sincerity. She fills all offices with dignity and efficiency. She is a typical all-round student. Girls of her type can never be forgotten.

name: Cook, Everett E.
clubs: Winner, S. T. C. Tennis Singles, '27
Delegate from World Affairs Club to "International Relations Club" Convention at Atlanta, '27
description: Everett, the son of "Daddy Joe," is carefree, confident, and ambitious. His genial disposition, polished manners, and originality of thought have won for him the warm friendship of everyone. Everett, a natural leader and a good sport, will be remembered not only as a dreamer but also as a doer of things.
text: Everett, the son of "Daddy Joe," is carefree, confident, and ambitious. His genial disposition, polished manners, and originality of thought have won for him the warm friendship of everyone. Everett, a natural leader and a good sport, will be remembered not only as a dreamer but also as a doer of things.

name: Miller, Sarah Louise
description: To know Sarah is to be her friend and not to know her is to have a friendship circle incomplete. Her pleasing manner has won for her the confidence of both students and faculty. She is true-hearted, whole-hearted, faithful and kind, and her modesty is the grace of her soul.
text: To know Sarah is to be her friend and not to know her is to have a friendship circle incomplete. Her pleasing manner has won for her the confidence of both students and faculty. She is true-hearted, whole-hearted, faithful and kind, and her modesty is the grace of her soul.

name: Bryant, Nancy B.
description: With her cheerful smile Nancy B. brings joy and happiness into the lives of others. She is an earnest, sincere student with an enviable class record.
text: With her cheerful smile Nancy B. brings joy and happiness into the lives of others. She is an earnest, sincere student with an enviable class record.

name: Owings, Fannie Ozell
description: Fannie is not known for her much speaking, but for her dependability and brilliant intellect when the real tests come. For she was just the quiet kind, Whose natures never vary; Like brooks that keep the summer mind Snow-hid in January.
text: Fannie is not known for her much speaking, but for her dependability and brilliant intellect when the real tests come. For she was just the quiet kind, Whose natures never vary; Like brooks that keep the summer mind Snow-hid in January.

name: May, George Edgar
description: May realizes that true living is uphill traveling, therefore he hopes to become a mountain climber. With him success is man's only excuse for being. He uses his past as a searchlight to illuminate his future.
text: May realizes that true living is uphill traveling, therefore he hopes to become a mountain climber. With him success is man's only excuse for being. He uses his past as a searchlight to illuminate his future.

name: Ryals, J. C.
description: J. C. came to us with "Much to learn—much to forget." At the S. T. C. he gained a vision of greater usefulness. A diligent student, a believer in fair play, and a pleasing personality—these attributes have won a place for J. C. in the hearts of both teachers and students.
text: J. C. came to us with "Much to learn—much to forget." At the S. T. C. he gained a vision of greater usefulness. A diligent student, a believer in fair play, and a pleasing personality—these attributes have won a place for J. C. in the hearts of both teachers and students.

name: Lyle, Rosalie
description: Her ambition throughout her college career has been to do good work, always to be on time, and to stay to the end. For application and industry she has no peer. The rule of her life is to make business her pleasure. In addition Rosalie has a sane, social attitude and is thoroughly human. Fortunate will be the community in which she lives and works.
text: Her ambition throughout her college career has been to do good work, always to be on time, and to stay to the end. For application and industry she has no peer. The rule of her life is to make business her pleasure. In addition Rosalie has a sane, social attitude and is thoroughly human. Fortunate will be the community in which she lives and works.

name: Flynt, Robinson
description: Flynt is always willing to serve his friends. Not content with merely doing his duty, he goes the "second mile."
text: Flynt is always willing to serve his friends. Not content with merely doing his duty, he goes the "second mile."

name: Hurst, G. G.
description: She is a wonder as wife, mother, school girl. She works with all her might and is loyal, wholesome and true. She knows more about managing a house and a husband than the guy that invented them. We love Miss Bette because we find in her all the qualities of a lady.
text: She is a wonder as wife, mother, school girl. She works with all her might and is loyal, wholesome and true. She knows more about managing a house and a husband than the guy that invented them. We love Miss Bette because we find in her all the qualities of a lady.

name: Thigpen, Herman A.
description: The confidence which meets every situation with a calm assurance is Herman's chief virtue. He has pleased many State Teachers College audiences with his vocal solos. We feel sure that he is destined to please larger and more critical audiences.
text: The confidence which meets every situation with a calm assurance is Herman's chief virtue. He has pleased many State Teachers College audiences with his vocal solos. We feel sure that he is destined to please larger and more critical audiences.

name: Ellis, Margaret Dorothy
clubs: Undergraduate Representative, Y. W. C. A., '28
description: Cheerful, helpful, capable, musical, artistic—a girl who serves everyone and loves those whom she serves—that's Margaret.
text: Cheerful, helpful, capable, musical, artistic—a girl who serves everyone and loves those whom she serves—that's Margaret.

name: Daniel, George Thomas
description: George is a sincere, quiet fellow who minds his own business. His sincerity has won for him many friends. He has been a conscientious student, and we wish for him the success he deserves.
text: George is a sincere, quiet fellow who minds his own business. His sincerity has won for him many friends. He has been a conscientious student, and we wish for him the success he deserves.

name: Lyon, Charlie Mary
motto: Man's love is of man's life a thing apart, 'Tis woman's whole existence.
text: Man's love is of man's life a thing apart, 'Tis woman's whole existence.

name: Griffin, Margaret
motto: "Thrills! Nothing great can be achieved without enthusiasm."
text: "Thrills! Nothing great can be achieved without enthusiasm."

name: Richardson, C. B.
motto: "Blessed be the man who, having nothing to say, does not talk."
text: "Blessed be the man who, having nothing to say, does not talk."

name: Stevens, Grace
motto: "Hang sorrow! Care will kill the cat."
text: "Hang sorrow! Care will kill the cat."

name: Ratliff, Ruby Elizabeth
motto: "Being natural, naturally pleases."
text: "Being natural, naturally pleases."

name: Camon, Neka
description: The light that lies in a woman's eyes, and lies, and lies, and lies.
text: The light that lies in a woman's eyes, and lies, and lies, and lies.

name: Deen, Addie Mae
motto: "Honor and shame from no condition rise; Act well your part—there all the honor lies."
text: "Honor and shame from no condition rise; Act well your part—there all the honor lies."

name: Myers, Katherine
motto: "Speak gently; it is better to rule by love than fear."
text: "Speak gently; it is better to rule by love than fear."

name: Russell, Mrs. R. L.
description: Mrs. Russell is an honor to the Junior Class, a lovable and delightful person to know.
text: Mrs. Russell is an honor to the Junior Class, a lovable and delightful person to know.

name: Burch, Henry Alfred
motto: "Where there's a will there's a way."
text: "Where there's a will there's a way."

name: Russell, G. H.
motto: "Of all the joys I'm able to recall, Being in love is the best of all."
description: Junior Class
text:
"Of all the joys I'm able to recall, Being in love is the best of all."
Junior Class

name: Furlow, Tina Lee
motto: "Silent is the starry sky."
description: Junior Class
text:
"Silent is the starry sky."
Junior Class

name: Mclean, Mable
motto: "A smooth and steadfast mind, Gentle thoughts and calm ways."
description: Junior Class
text:
"A smooth and steadfast mind, Gentle thoughts and calm ways."
Junior Class

name: Magee, Tillie Belle
motto: "A real woman is both competent and charming."
description: Junior Class
text:
"A real woman is both competent and charming."
Junior Class

name: Montgomery, Jewel
motto: "The voice so sweet, the word so fair, As some soft chime had stroked the air."
description: Junior Class
text:
"The voice so sweet, the word so fair, As some soft chime had stroked the air."
Junior Class

name: Oaks, Mary
motto: "You are won by her fair beauty; you are held by her noble qualities of true womanhood."
description: Junior Class S.T.C. '29
text:
"You are won by her fair beauty; you are held by her noble qualities of true womanhood."
Junior Class S.T.C. '29

name: Hulett, J. Ed
motto: "Give me true friends and music, and life will be a pleasure."
text: "Give me true friends and music, and life will be a pleasure."

name: Smith, Wilma Drucilla
motto:
"I wish of friendship
Blended with a heart of love;
An extract of goodness
From the heavens above."
text:
"I wish of friendship
Blended with a heart of love;
An extract of goodness
From the heavens above."

name: Russell, R. L.
motto: "Russell is a man who fulfills his duty faithfully."
text: "Russell is a man who fulfills his duty faithfully."

name: Hardin, Frances
motto:
"Quaint yet roguish,
Proud yet humble."
text:
"Quaint yet roguish,
Proud yet humble."

name: Neka Camon
motto: "I am not a politician, and my other habits are good."
text: "I am not a politician, and my other habits are good."

name: Frances Katherine Harrell
motto: "And still they gazed, and still the wonder grew, That one small head could carry all she knew."
text: "And still they gazed, and still the wonder grew, That one small head could carry all she knew."

name: Garvin H. Allen
motto: "Everybody comes within the circle of his understanding and sympathy."
text: "Everybody comes within the circle of his understanding and sympathy."

name: Maysel Holmiquist
motto: "Happiness reigns where smiles dwell."
text: "Happiness reigns where smiles dwell."

name: R. D. McLendon
motto: "Mac's popularity may be accounted for in one word—personality."
text: "Mac's popularity may be accounted for in one word—personality."

name: Mrs. J. B. George
motto: "A perfect woman, nobly planned, To warn, to comfort and command."
text: "A perfect woman, nobly planned, To warn, to comfort and command."
